content: I can't believe I'm saying this, but I actually miss the Bay Area sometimes. Moved to Pittsburgh a year ago for work.
What made you decide to move from the Bay Area to Pittsburgh?
I'm Morgan, by the way. I work as a fashion buyer for a luxury department store and got offered a remote position. The cost of living here is so much better.
That makes sense. What do you like to do in your free time now that you're in Pittsburgh?
I've gotten really into drone photography - the landscapes around here are stunning, especially in fall. I also picked up speedcubing again, which I haven't done since college.
That's interesting that you picked up speedcubing again. What got you back into it?
Honestly, it started as a way to cope with the stress of moving. I can solve a 3x3 in about 38 seconds now. I also DJ at a local bar on weekends - it's been a great way to meet people.
Wow, 38 seconds is impressive! What kind of music do you DJ?
Mostly house and techno, but I'll throw in some 80s tracks if the crowd seems right. I used to spin at warehouse parties in Oakland before I moved.
That sounds like a fun scene. Do you miss the Bay Area music scene?
Sometimes. The energy there was unmatched. But Pittsburgh has its own charm - I joined a local tennis league and have been playing twice a week.
Tennis is great exercise. How long have you been playing?
About six months. I'm still pretty new but improving. I also do origami when I need to unwind - it's surprisingly meditative after a long day of fashion buying.
Origami sounds like a nice creative outlet. Do you have a favorite design you've made?
I made this really intricate dragon last month that took me three hours. I'm thinking about taking a trip to Japan next spring - would love to see the origami masters in action.
Japan would be amazing for origami! Have you been before?

No, never. I went to Tokyo briefly for work a few years ago but only had 48 hours. This time I want to really explore. Maybe hit up some vintage fashion markets too - always looking for unique pieces for the store.
That sounds like a perfect combination of interests. When are you thinking of going?

Probably April or May. I need to check my work schedule - fashion week in Paris is in March so I'll be slammed then. Oh, and I almost forgot - I'm taking a drone photography course next month to improve my skills.
A drone photography course sounds perfect for capturing Japan. Best of luck with your planning and your upcoming course!
Thanks! I'm really looking forward to both. Maybe I'll finally get some shots good enough to sell as prints.
I'm sure you will! Your passion for both fashion and photography really comes through.
